title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement rgthomas@ucsd.edu. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contributing to zzpower
text: Thank interest contributing. document describes expected workflow proposing changes, reporting issues, submitting pull requests.

title: Contributing to zzpower
text: opening issue, please search existing issues https://github.com/rgt47/zzpower/issues confirm problem already reported. opening new issue, include: minimal reproducible example (reprex::reprex() preferred). Shiny-app issues, describe input values steps reproduce problem. output sessionInfo(). version zzpower use (packageVersion('zzpower')). expected behaviour observed behaviour.

title: Contributing to zzpower
text: non-trivial changes, please open issue first discuss approach. plugin/registry architecture well-defined extension points; brief design discussion saves rework.

title: Contributing to zzpower
text: zzpower’s registry pattern makes adding tests straightforward: Create test specification function (see R/power_test_registry.R) returns list id, name, pwr_function, effect-size methods, standardize() callback. Register spec get_power_test_registry(). Add specification tests tests/testthat/test-framework.R exercising effect-size methods edge cases. framework auto-generates UI tab, server logic, reports.

title: Contributing to zzpower
text: Fork repository create topic branch main. Install development dependencies. zzpower uses renv; restore pinned environment : Make changes. Keep commits focused; prefer many small commits one large one. Add update tests tests/testthat/. New exported functions require tests. Run devtools::document() regenerate man/ NAMESPACE. Run full check locally: check must pass errors, warnings, notes standard ‘New submission’ note. Update NEWS.md one-line bullet unreleased section. Open pull request main. Reference related issues.

title: Contributing to zzpower
text: Use native R pipe (|>); avoid %>% new code. Use <- assignment, never =. Use snake_case functions variables. Prefer implicit returns; reserve return() early exits. Document exported functions roxygen2. must @title, @description, @param, @return, @examples. Two-space indentation. Single quotes character literals. add ‘’ comments. Reserve comments non-obvious ‘’.

title: Contributing to zzpower
text: Tests use testthat 3rd edition. Run : coverage reports:

title: Contributing to zzpower
text: participating project, agree abide Code Conduct.

code: @Manual{,   title = {zzpower: Interactive Power Analysis Calculator for Clinical Trial Designs},   author = {Ronald G. Thomas},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.0.0},   url = {https://github.com/rgt47/zzpower}, }

title: zzpower
text: zzpower package provides Shiny application conducting power analysis sample size calculations two-group parallel clinical trial designs.

title: zzpower
text: Multiple Effect Size Methods: Support Cohen’s d, percentage reductions, difference change scores, treatment group changes Design Considerations: Accounts dropout rates, drop-rates, unequal group allocation ratios Interactive Visualizations: Real-time power curves detailed results tables Report Generation: Downloadable reports PDF, HTML, Word formats Advanced Settings: Configurable Type error rates one-sided vs two-sided testing

title: zzpower
text: development version may installed zzpower GitHub :

title: zzpower
text: launch interactive application: application open default web browser provide interactive interface : Sample Size Planning: Set total sample size expected dropout rates Effect Size Specification: Choose multiple methods specify expected treatment effect Advanced Configuration: Adjust randomization ratios, Type error rates, testing approaches Results Visualization: View interactive power curves detailed results tables Report Generation: Download reports multiple formats

title: zzpower
text: application supports four different approaches specifying effect sizes: Standard Deviation Units (Cohen’s d): Direct specification standardized effect sizes Percent Reduction: Effect size specified percentage reduction placebo Difference Change Scores: Absolute difference treatment groups outcome units Change Active Group: Specifying treatment group change directly

title: zzpower
text: Launch application launch_zzpower() Set total planned sample size using slider Specify expected dropout rate Select preferred effect size method Set range effect sizes evaluate Review power curve identify detectable effect size 80% power Download report documenting power analysis

Cluster-Randomized Log-rank Specification — create_cluster_logrank_spec
text: Two-group cluster-randomized trial survival outcome. Schoenfeld formula per-arm effective event count, event_prob design effect (DE) inflate variance.

title: Cluster-Randomized Log-rank Specification — create_cluster_logrank_spec
Cluster-Randomized Two-Proportion Specification — create_cluster_prop_spec
text: Two-group cluster-randomized trial binary outcome. Cohen's h per-arm effective sample size; design effect inflates variance via DE = 1 + (m_bar - 1) x ICC.

title: Cluster-Randomized Two-Proportion Specification — create_cluster_prop_spec
Cluster-Randomized RCT Specification (continuous outcome) — create_cluster_rct_spec
text: Two-group cluster-randomized trial continuous outcome. Uses Cohen's d per-arm effective sample size, design effect DE = 1 + (m_bar - 1) x ICC inflates variance. user enters total participants (across arms); power function consumes effective N = N / DE.

title: Cluster-Randomized RCT Specification (continuous outcome) — create_cluster_rct_spec
Cluster-Randomized RCT Specification (continuous outcome) — create_cluster_rct_spec
text: Modeled cluster-RCT design NCI sample R01 R01CA177592 (Mohile et al., reducing chemotherapy toxicity older adults; see grant-proposals-sample-size-practice.md Sec.1.4 exemplar paragraph). NIH cluster-randomized trials require adjustment per "special methods required" clause trials randomize groups.

what: Build a calc_context for a single power calculation — .build_calc_context
title: Build a calc_context for a single power calculation — .build_calc_context
text: Bundles inputs, outputs, provenance, metadata one power/sample-size calculation single named list. Every grant-writing artifact (Gap 1 methods paragraph, Gap 2 table builder, Gap 9 reproducibility script, Gap 3 multi-aim aggregator) reads values calc_context. Building context inputs consuming many places replaces otherwise eleven specs x five output-paste sites.

title: Build a calc_context for a single power calculation — .build_calc_context
code: .build_calc_context(   test_spec,   sample_sizes,   effect_size,   effect_size_std = NA_real_,   effect_method = NULL,   effect_params = list(),   target_power = NULL,   achieved_power = NULL,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = "two.sided",   effect_source = "",   effect_doi = "",   sensitivity_factor = NULL,   include_sex_paragraph = TRUE )

title: Build a calc_context for a single power calculation — .build_calc_context
text: test_spec test spec (one elements `get_power_test_registry()`). sample_sizes Output spec's `sample_size_calc()`, already canonicalized via `.canonicalize_sample_sizes()`. effect_size Numeric scalar effect size native scale chosen `effect_method`. effect_size_std Numeric scalar effect size standardized scale (Cohen's d, h, f, analogous). effect_method Character string identifying entry `test_spec$effect_size_methods` selected. effect_params Named list method-dependent parameters (e.g. `sd0`, `p2`, `baseline`). target_power Target power threshold (sample-size mode) NULL (power mode). achieved_power Achieved power proposed N (power mode) NULL (sample-size mode). alpha Type error rate. alternative `"two.sided"` `"one.sided"`. effect_source, effect_doi Provenance strings effect-size assumption (Gap 5). Default empty. sensitivity_factor Numeric multiplier conservative-effect sentence methods paragraph (Gap 6). include_sex_paragraph Logical; whether sex--bio paragraph appended (Gap 12).

title: Build a calc_context for a single power calculation — .build_calc_context
text: Named list calc_context shape.

title: Build a calc_context for a single power calculation — .build_calc_context
text: structural shape consumed programmatic API (`power_calc()`, Gap 10) Wave 2 generators. Field values populated earlier waves treated ground truth; fields populated later waves default NULL .

title: Canonicalize a sample-size calculation result — .canonicalize_sample_sizes
text: Converts per-arm enrolled counts plus dropout rate canonical four-layer record (`n_per_arm_evaluable`, `n_per_arm_enrolled`, `n_total_evaluable`, `n_total_enrolled`) every grant-writing artifact (Gaps 1, 2, 3, 9) reads . Back-compat scalars (`n`, `n1`, `n2`, `n1_itt`, `n2_itt`, `k`) populated consumers read legacy keys keep working unchanged.

title: Canonicalize a sample-size calculation result — .canonicalize_sample_sizes
Canonicalize a sample-size calculation result — .canonicalize_sample_sizes
text: per_arm_enrolled Numeric vector enrolled (pre-dropout) counts per arm. Length 1 single-sample tests, 2 two-arm tests, k ANOVA-style designs. dropout Expected dropout rate [0, 1). arm_labels Optional character vector arm names display. Defaults "Sample" one arm, "Group " otherwise.

title: Canonicalize a sample-size calculation result — .canonicalize_sample_sizes
text: Named list canonical fields plus legacy shims.

what: Render a methods-section paragraph from a calc_context — .render_methods_paragraph
title: Render a methods-section paragraph from a calc_context — .render_methods_paragraph
text: Produces Glueck-Muller-shaped paragraph pastes NIH proposal's Statistical Design Power attachment ICH E9 Sec. 3.5 sample-size statement. Composes seven sentences: (1) test + outcome, (2) effect-size assumption citation, (3) alpha + power + required N, (4) dropout inflation, (5) sensitivity sentence (ICH E9 Sec. 3.5; `sensitivity_factor` < 1), (6) software citation, (7) sex--biological-variable paragraph (NIH rigor; `include_sex_paragraph`).

title: Render a runnable reproducibility R script — .render_repro_script
text: pwr-backed tests script calls `pwr::pwr.<test>(...)` directly literal numeric arguments. zzpower's custom helpers (logrank_power, mcnemar_power, mixed_model_power, trend_power) script calls `zzpower::<helper>(...)` – exported precisely script works.

title: Solve for required N to achieve a target power — .required_n
text: test_spec registry entry. target_power Target power threshold (e.g. 0.80). effect_size_std Standardized effect size. design_params Named list design parameters consumed spec's `sample_size_calc()` (e.g. `dropout`, `allocation`). alpha Type error rate. alternative `"two.sided"` `"one.sided"`. n_lo, n_hi Bisection bounds total enrolled sample size.

title: Solve for required N to achieve a target power — .required_n
text: Smallest total enrolled N within [n_lo, n_hi]   achieves least `target_power`, `NA_real_` found.

title: Log-rank Test Power (Schoenfeld Formula) — logrank_power
text: Computes power log-rank test comparing two survival curves. Uses Schoenfeld (1981) formula based total expected events. Parameters n1 n2 represent expected events per group (raw sample sizes), event probability applied sample_size_calc values reach function.

title: Log-rank Test Power (Schoenfeld Formula) — logrank_power
Log-rank Test Power (Schoenfeld Formula) — logrank_power
text: h Standardized effect size: log(hazard ratio) n1 Expected events group 1 n2 Expected events group 2 sig.level Significance level alternative "two.sided" "one.sided"

title: Log-rank Test Power (Schoenfeld Formula) — logrank_power
text: List power element

title: Log-rank Test Power (Schoenfeld Formula) — logrank_power
text: Schoenfeld DA (1981). asymptotic properties nonparametric tests comparing survival distributions. Biometrika, 68(1), 316-319.

title: McNemar Test Power (Connor 1987) — mcnemar_power
text: Computes power McNemar's test comparing paired binary outcomes using normal approximation Connor (1987). effect size h represents difference discordant proportions (p10 - p01), n total number pairs. overall discordant rate (p10 + p01) folded standardized effect size spec's standardize function.

title: McNemar Test Power (Connor 1987) — mcnemar_power
McNemar Test Power (Connor 1987) — mcnemar_power
text: n Number pairs d Standardized effect size: |p10 - p01| / sqrt(p_disc - (p10-p01)^2) sig.level Significance level alternative "two.sided" "one.sided"

title: McNemar Test Power (Connor 1987) — mcnemar_power
text: List power element

title: McNemar Test Power (Connor 1987) — mcnemar_power
text: Connor RJ (1987). Sample size testing differences proportions paired-sample design. Biometrics, 43(1), 207-211.

what: Longitudinal Mixed Model Power (Diggle et al. 2002) — mixed_model_power
title: Longitudinal Mixed Model Power (Diggle et al. 2002) — mixed_model_power
text: Computes power two-group comparison slopes linear mixed model equally spaced time points compound symmetry correlation. Based formula Diggle et al. (2002, p. 29).

title: Longitudinal Mixed Model Power (Diggle et al. 2002) — mixed_model_power
Longitudinal Mixed Model Power (Diggle et al. 2002) — mixed_model_power
text: n Per-group sample size d Standardized slope effect size: delta * sqrt(S_tt / (2 * sigma^2 * (1 - rho))) sig.level Significance level alternative "two.sided" "one.sided"

title: Longitudinal Mixed Model Power (Diggle et al. 2002) — mixed_model_power
text: List power element

title: Longitudinal Mixed Model Power (Diggle et al. 2002) — mixed_model_power
text: standardized effect size d incorporates slope difference, residual variance, within-subject correlation, number time points. parameter n represents per-group sample size.

title: Longitudinal Mixed Model Power (Diggle et al. 2002) — mixed_model_power
text: Diggle PJ, Heagerty P, Liang K-Y, Zeger SL (2002). Analysis Longitudinal Data, 2nd ed. Oxford University Press, p. 29.

title: Programmatic power calculation — power_calc
text: Compute power (required N) single test R, without launching Shiny app. companion `power_table()` tabular sweeps.

title: Programmatic power calculation — power_calc
code: power_calc(   test,   sample_size = NULL,   effect_size,   effect_method = NULL,   target_power = NULL,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = "two.sided",   ... )

title: Programmatic power calculation — power_calc
text: test Character test id (one `names(get_power_test_registry())`) test_spec list returned registry. sample_size Total enrolled sample size. Optional sample-size mode (`target_power` supplied) – left NULL function solves required N via bisection. effect_size Effect size native scale chosen `effect_method` (e.g. raw difference `"difference"`, hazard ratio `"hazard_ratio"`, Cohen's d `"cohens_d"`). effect_method Character; one `test_spec$effect_size_methods`. Default first. target_power Target power threshold. supplied function solves required N; otherwise computes achieved power `sample_size`. alpha Type error rate. alternative `"two.sided"` `"one.sided"`. ... Additional design parameters passed spec's `sample_size_calc()` (e.g. `dropout`, `allocation`, `ratio`, `event_prob`, `n_groups`) `standardize()` (e.g. `sd0`, `p2`, `baseline`, `sigma`, `correlation`).

title: Programmatic power calculation — power_calc
text: `calc_context` named list (see `.build_calc_context`).

title: Programmatic power calculation — power_calc
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Power at fixed N power_calc("ttest_2groups",            sample_size = 100, effect_size = 0.5,            effect_method = "cohens_d")  # Required N at target power power_calc("ttest_2groups",            target_power = 0.80, effect_size = 0.5,            effect_method = "cohens_d") } # }

what: Sample-size sensitivity table for a test — power_table
title: Sample-size sensitivity table for a test — power_table
text: Produces Sec.2.1 Layout 1 sensitivity table: rows = effect-size grid, columns = required N power threshold (typically 0.80 0.90). artifact often pasted directly NIH proposal.

title: Sample-size sensitivity table for a test — power_table
code: power_table(   test,   effect_grid = NULL,   power_thresholds = c(0.8, 0.9),   effect_method = NULL,   alpha = 0.05,   alternative = "two.sided",   ... )

title: Sample-size sensitivity table for a test — power_table
text: test Character test id (one `names(get_power_test_registry())`) test_spec list returned registry. effect_grid Numeric vector effect sizes native scale. NULL, uses spec's `default_effect_grid` chosen `effect_method`. power_thresholds Numeric vector power thresholds table columns. Defaults `c(0.80, 0.90)`. effect_method Character; one `test_spec$effect_size_methods`. Default first. alpha Type error rate. alternative `"two.sided"` `"one.sided"`. ... Additional design parameters passed spec's `sample_size_calc()` (e.g. `dropout`, `allocation`, `ratio`, `event_prob`, `n_groups`) `standardize()` (e.g. `sd0`, `p2`, `baseline`, `sigma`, `correlation`).

title: Sample-size sensitivity table for a test — power_table
text: Tidy `data.frame` columns `effect_size`,   `effect_size_std`, one `n_total_enrolled_at_<power>`   column per power threshold (plus `n_total_evaluable_at_*`).

title: Sample-size sensitivity table for a test — power_table
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ power_table("ttest_2groups",             effect_grid = c(0.20, 0.50, 0.80),             effect_method = "cohens_d",             power_thresholds = c(0.80, 0.90)) } # }

what: zzpower: Interactive Power Analysis Calculator for Clinical Trials — zzpower-package
title: zzpower: Interactive Power Analysis Calculator for Clinical Trials — zzpower-package
text: zzpower package provides interactive 'Shiny' application covering eleven statistical tests via registry/plugin architecture: two-group, paired, one-sample t-tests; two-proportion comparisons; Pearson correlation; log-rank survival; Fisher's exact test; Cochran-Armitage trend; one-way ANOVA; McNemar's test paired proportions; basic linear mixed-model power calculation. test supports multiple effect-size specifications, design parameters (alpha, allocation, dropout), downloadable reports.

title: zzpower: Interactive Power Analysis Calculator for Clinical Trials — zzpower-package
text: 'Shiny' application power analysis sample size calculations across catalogue statistical tests common clinical trial design.

title: zzpower: Interactive Power Analysis Calculator for Clinical Trials — zzpower-package
text: Eleven statistical tests via     get_power_test_registry registry, rendered     generic UI/server factory pair     (create_generic_test_ui,     create_generic_test_server) Multiple effect-size methods per test (Cohen's d, h, f,     hazard ratio, odds ratio, percent reduction, etc.) Sample-size power solve modes: compute power     given N N required target power Interactive power curves reference lines     formatted result tables (DT) Downloadable reports (text HTML) summarising study     design power calculation Design considerations: dropout, drop-, allocation     ratio, one-sided vs two-sided testing

title: zzpower: Interactive Power Analysis Calculator for Clinical Trials — zzpower-package
text: launch interactive application, run:

title: zzpower: Interactive Power Analysis Calculator for Clinical Trials — zzpower-package
text: Cohen, J. (1988). Statistical Power Analysis Behavioral Sciences (2nd ed.). Lawrence Erlbaum Associates. Schoenfeld, D. . (1983). Sample-size formula proportional- hazards regression model. Biometrics, 39(2), 499-503. Champely, S. (2020). pwr: Basic Functions Power Analysis. R package.

title: zzpower: Interactive Power Analysis Calculator for Clinical Trials — zzpower-package
text: Ronald G. Thomas

title: zzpower v1.0.0 (in development)
text: R CMD check ---tests reports Status: OK (0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes) built tarball. full check tests included also passes. Typst PDF backend removed. -demand-install prompt Typst rendering path dropped typst R package CRAN, forced one : () CRAN policy violation (Suggests package mainstream repositories) (b) static-analysis-evading code obfuscation. PDF reports now render exclusively via rmarkdown’s xelatex pipeline (existing fallback). Roughly 220 lines removed R/generic_server_factory.R. feature can reinstated typst published CRAN. Test scaffolding cleaned . Six unprefixed internal- function calls inst/tinytest/test_module_server.R (e.g. bare .render_methods_paragraph(ctx)) updated zzpower::: prefixes. bare calls working devtools::test() (exposes namespace via load_all()) failing R CMD check (uses library(), hiding internals). Stale tests/testthat/ scaffolding tests/testthat.R removed (package migrated tinytest earlier release). Roxygen orphan-block fix. Five files file-level roxygen header blocks (e.g. #' Power Test Registry) function attached. roxygen2 silently merged next function’s docs, producing cascade malformed \keyword{...} entries (one per word merged description) wrong \title{}. Files: R/power_helpers.R, R/power_test_registry.R, R/proportion_helpers.R, R/generic_ui_builder.R, R/generic_server_factory.R. hoisted @importFrom directives moved R/zzpower-package.R belong. stripped header blocks redundant boilerplate, real documentation. Vignette artifact removed. vignettes/quickstart.pdf deleted; presence triggered find_vignette_product() trap zzedc (.pdf selected .html tie-mtime case, stripped .Rbuildignore ordering, dropping HTML output). .gitignore already covered vignettes/*.pdf. DESCRIPTION corrections. R (>= 4.0.0) -> R (>= 4.1.0) (package uses native pipe |>); LazyData: true removed (data/ directory exists). Top-level cleanup. Placeholder LICENSE file deleted (License: GPL (>= 3) entry need ; CRAN flagged file unmentioned). Hidden .zzcollab directory added .Rbuildignore. Non-ASCII middle-dot R/launch_zzpower.R:147 swapped ASCII |.

title: zzpower v1.0.0 (in development)
text: roadmap’s Gap 3 calls study-level table assembling multiple Specific Aims one §2.5 Layout 4 table. release ships algorithmic core exported R functions; Shiny nav-panel + URL-bookmarking UI deferred v1.1.x (bulk 24h estimate).

title: zzpower v1.0.0 (in development)
text: multi_aim_study(study_name) — initialise empty study object. Returns multi_aim_study (S3) carrying optional metadata aims list. add_aim(study, ctx, name, outcome) — append one calc_context (power_calc()) per call. Accumulates aims study. format_multi_aim_df(study) — produce tidy data frame columns Aim, Outcome, Test, Effect size, Alpha, Power, N evaluable, N enrolled, Binding. Binding column flags row whose enrolled N largest — aim drives overall study size. multi_aim_markdown(study) — render table paste-ready Markdown block §2.3-style caption, “Aim *” asterisk binding row, one-line legend . multi_aim_csv(study, file) — return table CSV string (file = NULL) write disk. Reviewers paste straight Excel.

what: Worked example: reconstructing the Mohile R01 multi-aim table
title: zzpower v1.0.0 (in development)
code: study <- multi_aim_study(study_name = "Reducing Chemo Toxicity") study <- add_aim(study,   power_calc("cluster_prop", target_power = 0.80,              effect_size = -0.13, effect_method = "difference",              m_cluster = 39, icc = 0.10, baseline = 0.46,              dropout = 0.10),   name = "Primary",   outcome = "Grade 3-5 chemo toxicity within 3 months") study <- add_aim(study,   power_calc("logrank", target_power = 0.80,              effect_size = 0.511, effect_method = "hazard_ratio",              event_prob = 0.7, dropout = 0.10),   name = "Secondary 1",   outcome = "6-month survival") multi_aim_markdown(study)

title: zzpower v1.0.0 (in development)
text: Shiny nav-panel “Multi-aim study” enableBookmarking('url') shareable URL state, “Add aim study” buttons test panel, -app delete/rename, etc. programmatic API ships data structures rendering logic nav-panel consume; deferred work pure UI plumbing.

title: zzpower v0.7.0 (in development)
text: Three new test specifications: cluster_rct (continuous outcome via Cohen’s d), cluster_prop (binary outcome via Cohen’s h), cluster_logrank (survival outcome via Schoenfeld formula). adds m_cluster (mean cluster size) icc (intracluster correlation) parameters; design effect DE = 1 + (m̄ − 1) × ICC inflates variance, equivalent reducing per-arm effective sample size N / DE. Methodologically standard (Donner & Klar 2000); fills NIH “special methods required” gap cluster-randomized trials. Reference design: NCI Mohile R01CA177592. Schoenfeld back-compat n1/n2 cluster_logrank encode participants × event_prob / DE (events cluster shrinkage), mirroring pattern non-cluster logrank spec uses events. Total registry size grows 11 14 specs.

title: zzpower v0.7.0 (in development)
text: anova_oneway gains n_pairwise_contrasts input. set > 1 spec’s new effective_alpha hook divides α contrast count solving omnibus F-test. omnibus F require Bonferroni correction; readout conservative (N strictly needed omnibus) supports protocol-level commitment Bonferroni post-hoc pairwise comparisons. methods paragraph annotates adjustment -line: α=0.0167 (Bonferroni-adjusted 0.05). reproducibility R script uses adjusted α directly. Generic effective_alpha = function(input, alpha) registry hook plumbed power_calc() .required_n(). specs inherit identity behavior; future specs can declare multiplicity adjustments without engine changes.

title: zzpower v0.7.0 (in development)
text: ttest_2groups gains hypothesis_type (radio: superiority / non_inferiority) ni_margin (numeric, conditional NI selection). NI selected, standardize() function shifts standardized effect margin power computed (true_effect + margin) / sd. matches FDA convention one-sided α=0.025 critical value two-sided α=0.05. Latent bug fix .compute_power: zzpower’s internal "one.sided" label now mapped "greater" passed pwr::pwr.* functions, accept "two.sided"/"less"/"greater". bug previously masked superiority tests typically use two-sided defaults; one-sided + NI workflow surfaced . Deferred v0.7.x: non-inferiority prop_2groups (Cohen’s h margin conversion) logrank (log-HR margin) needs careful per-scale margin handling left follow-work. Equivalence (TOST) also deferred.

title: zzpower v0.6.1 (in development)
text: Reproducibility script appended every report. four formats (markdown, text, HTML, PDF via Typst, Word via rmarkdown) now include “Reproducibility script” section reproduces headline calculation fresh R session. library(pwr) seven pwr-backed tests; library(zzpower) four custom helpers (logrank, McNemar, mixed model, Cochran-Armitage trend). script uses literal numeric arguments — Shiny state required. Custom helpers exported. logrank_power, mcnemar_power, mixed_model_power, trend_power now part package’s public API reproducibility script runs. Previously @keywords internal . Argument order matches function signature. rendered call places arguments order formals() declares (e.g. d, n1, n2, sig.level, alternative) rather alphabetic insertion order, script reads naturally. Per-spec repro_call field populated. Wave 1 reserved repro_call NULL; Wave 3 fills qualified function name ("pwr::pwr.t2n.test", "zzpower::logrank_power", etc.) eleven registry entries. .report_data_to_ctx() helper. Reshapes existing report-data list calc_context renderer can share one engine across methods-paragraph (Wave 2) reproducibility-script (Wave 3) generators.

title: zzpower v0.6.0 (in development)
text: release ships two artifacts grant writers paste directly: methods-section paragraph (Gap 1) sensitivity table (Gap 2). consume calc_context contract built Wave 1.

title: zzpower v0.6.0 (in development)
text: New “Methods paragraph” card every test panel renders Glueck-Muller-shaped paragraph NIH Statistical Design Power attachment ICH E9 §3.5 sample-size statement. Composed seven sentences: planned analysis, effect-size assumption citation, alpha + power + N (per-arm phrased multi-arm designs), dropout inflation, sensitivity scenario (ICH E9 §3.5), software citation, sex--biological-variable paragraph (NIH rigor; toggleable). S3 phrasing flips “achieved power X%” (power mode) “X evaluable participants required” (sample-size mode). “Copy” button uses navigator.clipboard.writeText put paragraph clipboard; button briefly reads “Copied” confirmation. paragraph reflects lower end effect-size slider — conservative assumption, produces largest N. matches NIH convention reviewers assume smallest effect user’s range. Effect-size phrasing humanised per method (cohens_d → “Cohen’s d”, hazard_ratio → “hazard ratio”, correlation → “correlation coefficient r”, eleven methods).

title: zzpower v0.6.0 (in development)
text: New “Sensitivity table” card every test panel renders §2.1 Layout 1 sample-size table: rows = effect-size grid, columns = required N 80% 90% power, evaluable (analyzed) enrolled (post-dropout-inflation) layers. Default rows seed spec’s default_effect_grid (Cohen’s small/medium/large d, f, r; reasonable per-method grids proportion difference, odds ratio, hazard ratio, etc.). Effect-size column editable: double-click cell override; four N columns recompute via power_table(). columns read-. Two download handlers ship CSV Markdown formats. Markdown caption follows §2.3 conventions (inputs vary / held fixed / formula citation). new .df_to_markdown(df, caption) helper produces table body.

title: zzpower v0.6.0 (in development)
text: Twelve new assertions cover methods-paragraph renderer (Test name, citation, alpha, “evaluable participants”, dropout, sensitivity sentence, software, sex paragraph), sex-toggle -path, sensitivity-factor -path, Shiny output reactive, sensitivity-table reactive (default 3 rows cohens_d grid; required-N monotonicity effect; 90% > 80% N), markdown formatter. Total assertions rose 174 197.

title: zzpower v0.5.0 (in development)
text: release lands structural plumbing grant-writing roadmap (Gaps 4, 5, 6, 10, 12 docs/grant-proposals-sample-size-practice.md). new headline artifact ships version — methods-paragraph generator, sensitivity table builder, reproducibility script export (Wave 2 / Wave 3) build top foundations.

title: zzpower v0.5.0 (in development)
text: eleven sample_size_calc() returns now share canonical shape n_per_arm_evaluable / n_per_arm_enrolled / n_total_evaluable / n_total_enrolled / n_arms / arm_labels / dropout, plus legacy n / n1 / n2 / k / n1_itt / n2_itt shims existing consumers (server module, report builders, headline value-boxes) read. new .canonicalize_sample_sizes() helper centralises enrolled--evaluable conversion. Behaviour change: ten specs previously dropout parameter now expose Dropout Rate slider default 0.10. Power curves tests now ~10% lower unless user dials dropout 0. aligns every test existing ttest_2groups convention matches NIH-realistic default 10% loss follow-. Logrank’s back-compat n1/n2 continue report expected events (value Schoenfeld formula consumes); canonical n_per_arm_* fields report participants first time.

title: zzpower v0.5.0 (in development)
text: New exported power_calc(test, sample_size, effect_size, effect_method, target_power, alpha, alternative, ...) returns calc_context named list — every grant-writing artifact reads values single object. Supplying target_power instead sample_size triggers bisection required-N answer. New exported power_table(test, effect_grid, power_thresholds, ...) returns §2.1 Layout 1 sensitivity table tidy data.frame. Defaults Cohen small/medium/large spec’s default_effect_grid; 0.80 0.90 power thresholds column set box. Two free helpers (.compute_power, .required_n) now hold call-assembly logic Shiny module’s .power_at_n closure inlined; server keeps closure unchanged release, next refactor route shared helpers.

title: zzpower v0.5.0 (in development)
text: registry entry gains four new fields: formula_citation (one-liner canonical citation test’s formula — Cohen, Schoenfeld, Connor, Diggle, Cochran–Armitage applicable), default_effect_grid (per-method numeric grid power_table()), paragraph_template (NULL placeholder; populated Wave 2), repro_call (NULL placeholder; populated Wave 3). .build_calc_context() constructs calc_context test_spec + canonical sample sizes + provenance fields. contract every Wave 2/3 generator reads .

title: zzpower v0.5.0 (in development)
text: Advanced Settings accordion gains four new inputs Wave 2 methods-paragraph generator inject output: effect_source (citation text), effect_doi, sensitivity_factor (multiplier conservative-effect sentence per ICH E9 §3.5), include_sex_paragraph (NIH rigor toggle). inputs render affect output yet; rendered output Wave 2.

title: zzpower v0.5.0 (in development)
text: inst/tinytest/test_module_server.R gains 160 assertions: per-spec verification canonical N contract holds every entry registry, end--end smoke tests power_calc() power_table(). Total assertions rose 14 174.

title: zzpower v0.4.1 (2026-05-03)
text: PDF Word formats added Download Report card. PDF rendering uses Typst (via optional typst package) fast, -LaTeX-required output falls back xelatex via rmarkdown::pdf_document() Typst installed. Word uses rmarkdown::word_document() (pandoc). user selects PDF without typst installed, app shows -per-session modal offering install package download Typst CLI binary. four report formats (text, HTML, PDF, Word) now include package citation footer plain-text form, drawn new inst/CITATION file. citation reads “Thomas, R.G. (2026). zzpower: …”

title: zzpower v0.4.1 (2026-05-03)
text: Power-curve plots now annotate 80 percent 90 percent power thresholds. 80 percent line gold dashed (primary); 90 percent line grey dotted (secondary), smaller marker label. y-axis renders percent (50 percent / 80 percent / …) rather 0.5 / 0.8. Sample-size plots gain “min N = …” annotation smallest required-N point. plotly experiment introduced mid-cycle rolled back; geom_label refactor produced retained strictly cleaner original annotate("label", ...) pattern.

title: zzpower v0.4.1 (2026-05-03)
text: Page footer added: version, source link, issue tracker, “cite” modal trigger, licence. Hero category grids landing page use breakpoint-aware column widths single-card categories longer leave 75 percent row empty small screens.

title: zzpower v0.4.1 (2026-05-03)
text: htmltools added Imports (used citation HTML escape report footer). typst added Suggests (optional, PDF reports).

title: zzpower v0.4.0 (2026-05-02)
text: hero-card launcher cards landing screen now keyboard accessible. Previously rendered <div> onclick handler, made unreachable tab navigation unannounced buttons screen readers. card now declares role = "button", tabindex = "0", aria-label, onkeydown handler triggers Enter Space, visible focus outline. inst/app/server.R longer runs code paths enhanced gsheets integration succeeds. (applicable zzpower; carry-note upstream review.)

title: zzpower v0.4.0 (2026-05-02)
text: Headline value boxes. test panel now shows three bslib::value_box tiles power curve results table, surfacing answer user came : effect size 80% power, maximum power tested range, total sample size (power-solve mode); smallest largest required N target power (sample-size-solve mode). Sidebar reorganised accordion panels. sidebar now uses bslib::accordion three panels (‘Sample Size & Design’, ‘Effect Size’, ‘Advanced Settings’). Advanced Settings panel closed default; two open. Replaces tall stack <hr>-separated sections. Structured study-summary card. Study Summary card previously rendered verbatimTextOutput (monospace, ASCII). now <dl> definition list semantic Bootstrap styling. Parameter tooltips. Every input now shows description field test registry hover tooltip via info-icon next label. metadata existed registry never rendered. Validation messages. inputs invalid, plot table cards now show ‘Resolve input issues shown sidebar see results.’ instead going blank (shiny::validate(shiny::need(...)) replaces silent req()). Result-table formatting. DT results table now uses formatRound effect-size standardized columns formatPercentage power column. Pagination chrome hidden table fits 16 rows. Busy indicators. shiny::useBusyIndicators() (Shiny 1.9.0+) now provides automatic recalculating glyphs global pulse reactive computation.

title: zzpower v0.4.0 (2026-05-02)
text: Caching. power_results sample_size_results reactives now wrapped shiny::bindCache(), keyed inputs affect calculation. Repeated parameter combinations (common user drags slider back previous value) return instantly.

title: zzpower v0.4.0 (2026-05-02)
text: Removed ~1,800 lines dead legacy code. Deleted R/server_logic.R, R/ui_components.R, R/server_proportions.R, R/ui_proportions.R, R/report_generation.R, plus four test files exercised (test_server_logic.R, test_ui_components.R, test_proportions.R, test_validation.R). replaced registry/factory architecture (generic_ui_builder.R, generic_server_factory.R, power_test_registry.R) kept alive tests. DT API modernised. Replaced deprecated DT::dataTableOutput DT::renderDataTable current DT::DTOutput DT::renderDT aliases. Pipe convention. Removed magrittr Imports; surviving %>% pipes inside deleted legacy files. code now uses native |>. shiny floor. Bumped >= 1.7.0 >= 1.9.0. Required useBusyIndicators(); covers bindCache() (1.6.0) ExtendedTask (1.8.0) reach. Public module API. create_generic_test_ui() create_generic_test_server() now exported. de-facto public surface (used launch_zzpower()) previously marked @keywords internal. Updated package-level docstring. Now reflects eleven registered tests rather just original two-group parallel design.

title: zzpower v0.4.0 (2026-05-02)
text: testServer() reactivity tests. New test file inst/tinytest/test_module_server.R exercises four power-function shapes registry (n1+n2 / single-n / proportions via Cohen’s h / survival via custom helper) end end shiny::testServer(). Total test count rose ~15 assertions.

title: zzpower v0.3.0
text: Initial public release.
